summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the discriminant of 3x^2 + 2x - 5 = 0?

The discriminant is b^2 - 4ac = (2)^2 - 4(3)(-5) = 4 + 60 = 64. Since 64 > 0, there are two distinct real solutions.

What is the discriminant of x^2 + 3x + 5 = 0?

Discriminant = 9 - 20 = -11. A negative discriminant means there are no real solutions, only two complex conjugate solutions.

What is the quadratic formula?

The quadratic formula x = (-b +/- sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)) / (2a) gives the solutions to any quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0 when a is not zero.

What is the discriminant of a quadratic equation?

The discriminant D = b^2 - 4ac is the expression under the square root in the quadratic formula. Its value determines whether the solutions are real or complex, and distinct or repeated.

What is the 'plus or minus' symbol (+/-) in the quadratic formula?

The +/- symbol means you compute two separate values: one using addition (-b + sqrt(D))/(2a) and one using subtraction (-b - sqrt(D))/(2a). These give the two solutions of the quadratic.

A rectangular pool's length is 3 meters more than twice its width. The area is 65 square meters. What is the width?

Let w = width: w(2w + 3) = 65, so 2w^2 + 3w - 65 = 0. Using the formula: w = (-3 +/- sqrt(9 + 520))/4 = (-3 +/- 23)/4. Taking the positive: w = 20/4 = 5 meters.
